top_stories.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
top_stories.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.
top_stories.author: Kate Park
top_stories.content: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

top_stories.title: How Amazon Bedrock catches AI-generated phishing
top_stories.description: Social engineering through phishing remains one of the most common tactics for launching cyberattacks. AI-generated phishing email messages now pose a new challenge for security teams managing email systems, significantly raising the risk because of their advanced sophistication. Modern social engineers use generative AI and open source intelligence (OSINT) to craft thousands of unique messages […]
top_stories.author: Radha Panchap

top_stories.title: Claude in Microsoft Foundry is now generally available
top_stories.description: Claude in Microsoft Foundry is now generally available, hosted on Azure, and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra, giving teams a faster path from agent experimentation to production. The post Claude in Microsoft Foundry is now generally available appeared first on Microsoft Azure Blog.
top_stories.author: Steve Sweetman
top_stories.content: Claude in Microsoft Foundry is now generally available, hosted on Azure, and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra, giving teams a faster path from agent experimentation to production. The post Claude in Microsoft Foundry is now generally available appeared first on Microsoft Azure Blog.

top_stories.title: Run MiniMax models on Amazon Bedrock
top_stories.description: In this post, we walk through how to get started with MiniMax models on Amazon Bedrock, including the capabilities supported by these models, the service tiers available, how on-demand inference scales to handle your workloads, and the different APIs you can use to access them. Using these models, customers can build agentic applications, long-context document analysis pipelines, and software engineering workflows, all backed by the security and operational guarantees of AWS.
top_stories.author: Zohreh Norouzi
top_stories.content: In this post, we walk through how to get started with MiniMax models on Amazon Bedrock, including the capabilities supported by these models, the service tiers available, how on-demand inference scales to handle your workloads, and the different APIs you can use to access them. Using these models, customers can build agentic applications, long-context document analysis pipelines, and software engineering workflows, all backed by the security and operational guarantees of AWS.
